major_findings.problem: Multiple scene prompts used environment handles such as anchor rail, supervisor glass, crawl throat, seal assembly, dry-operation support station, and manifold without enough standalone visible geometry.
major_findings.why_it_matters: A general image model only sees the assembled prompt; labels that are meaningful to Aetheria readers are not drawable unless translated into materials, shape, scale, lights, panels, pipes, rails, doors, and surrounding space.
major_findings.evidence: Examples included prompts centered on 'dry-side anchor rail', 'AU supervisor glass', 'Kappa access throat', and 'Kappa seal assembly' before visible structures were expanded.
major_findings.fix_direction: Expand every base image prompt so environment handles are backed by visible geometry and materials.

minor_findings.problem: Orrin was represented by a cracked mug but still carried a visible character ref policy in the visible_characters list.
minor_findings.why_it_matters: Prompt assembly should not include character refs for absent or object-represented characters, or the image model may draw a body that should not be in frame.
minor_findings.evidence: Orrin Dax default stance was 'absent or off-frame, represented by cracked mug'.
minor_findings.fix_direction: Remove absent character from visible_characters and represent him through a concrete object callback.

suggested_revisions: When a real image-generation tool is wired in, preserve a rendered assembled-prompt receipt per visual_scene_id so reviewer audits can inspect the exact text sent to the image model.
acceptance_notes: Post-revision audit checked 29 visual scenes for common unresolved environment handles and found zero failures.

Follow-up segmentation pass split major tonal/power-geometry turns into dedicated visual anchors, including Ilya's arrival after Nara's refusal and BioElevate legal's liability reframe after the initial AU/worker classification split.
The global Dreams-style cue remains wired into prompt assembly.
Visual continuity notes remain tooling constraints and are not part of assembled prompt text.
Character refs are limited to characters actually visible in a rendered frame.
